lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Masters, play here; I will content your pains;

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Something that’s brief; and bid “Good morrow, general.”

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: Why masters, have your instruments been in Naples, that they speak i’ the nose thus?

lines.speaker: FIRST MUSICIAN
lines.text: How, sir, how!

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: Are these, I pray you, wind-instruments?

lines.speaker: FIRST MUSICIAN
lines.text: Ay, marry, are they, sir.

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: O, thereby hangs a tail.

lines.speaker: FIRST MUSICIAN
lines.text: Whereby hangs a tale, sir?

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: Marry, sir, by many a wind-instrument that I know. But, masters, here’s

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: money for you: and the general so likes your music, that he

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: desires you, for love’s sake, to make no more noise with it.

lines.speaker: FIRST MUSICIAN
lines.text: Well, sir, we will not.

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: If you have any music that may not be heard, to’t again:

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: but, as they say to hear music the general does not greatly

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: care.

lines.speaker: FIRST MUSICIAN
lines.text: We have none such, sir.

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: Then put up your pipes in your bag, for I’ll away: go;

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: vanish into air; away!

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Dost thou hear, my honest friend?

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: No, I hear not your honest friend; I hear you.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Prithee, keep up thy quillets. There’s a poor piece of gold for

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: thee: if the gentlewoman that attends the general’s wife be stirring, tell

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: her there’s one Cassio entreats her a little favour of speech: wilt

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: thou do this?

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: She is stirring, sir: if she will stir hither, I shall seem

lines.speaker: CLOWN
lines.text: to notify unto her.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Do, good my friend.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: You have not been a-bed, then?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Why, no; the day had broke

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Before we parted. I have made bold, Iago,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: To send in to your wife: my suit to her

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Is, that she will to virtuous Desdemona

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Procure me some access.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I’ll send her to you presently;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And I’ll devise a mean to draw the Moor

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Out of the way, that your converse and business

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: May be more free.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: I humbly thank you for’t.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: I never knew

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: A Florentine more kind and honest.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Good morrow, good Lieutenant: I am sorry

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: For your displeasure; but all will sure be well.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: The general and his wife are talking of it;

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: And she speaks for you stoutly: the Moor replies,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: That he you hurt is of great fame in Cyprus,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: And great affinity, and that in wholesome wisdom

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: He might not but refuse you; but he protests he loves you

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: And needs no other suitor but his likings

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: To take the safest occasion by the front

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: To bring you in again.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Yet, I beseech you,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: If you think fit, or that it may be done,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Give me advantage of some brief discourse

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: With Desdemona alone.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Pray you, come in;

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I will bestow you where you shall have time

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: To speak your bosom freely.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: I am much bound to you.

glosses.phrase: masters, have your instruments been in Naples
glosses.definition: the Clown jokes that the musicians' instruments sound nasal, as if they caught a Neapolitan disease (syphilis was nicknamed 'the Naples bone-ache')
a bawdy quibble — with anatomical innuendo

annotations.title: The Clown as interval
annotations.body: Shakespeare inserts a brief comic interlude with a Clown between the scene of Cassio's fall and the temptation scene to come. The quibbles on wind instruments and Neapolitan ailments (syphilis was known in England as 'the Naples disease') buy an interval of lightness that makes what follows land harder. The Clown also scales the play's musical register down from Iago's songs in 2.3 to mundane morning jokes — a way of announcing that the pastoral island has resumed its ordinary life while the tragedy is quietly organizing itself in the next room.
sources: Robert H. Bell, *Shakespeare's Great Stage of Fools* (2011)

trials.prompt: What does the Clown joke about when discussing the musicians' instruments?
options: Their cost and the guild pricing system
That the instruments sound nasal, as if infected with 'the Naples disease' (syphilis)
That they are out of tune for the Venetian scale
That the musicians are all deaf

trials.prompt: Why does Emilia tell Cassio that Desdemona is already pleading his cause?
options: Because Othello has already agreed to reinstate him
Because Desdemona has reviewed evidence exonerating Cassio
Because her generous nature leads her to advocate for him even before being asked
Because Iago has asked her to lobby on Cassio's behalf
